sections.paragraph:
περιφέρω aor. ptc. fem. acc. sg. περιενέγκασαν 2 Macc 7:27 carry about, carry here and there

ⓐ lit., w. acc. (Eur., Pla. et al.; Plut., Mor. 331c; SIG 1169, 65f, a spearhead in the face; Josh 24:33a; 2 Macc 7:27; TestSol; Ar. 5:3; Tat.) the sick Mk 6:55. τὰ δεσμά the chains IEph 11:2; cp. IMg 1:2; ITr 12:2. τὴν νέκρωσιν τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ σώματι π. 2 Cor 4:10 (s. νέκρωσις 1).

ⓑ fig. (Epict. 2, 8, 12 θεὸν π.=carry God about within oneself; 2, 16, 33; TestSol 12:3 C οἴησιν (‘opinion’) περιφερόμενοι; Philo, Omnis Prob. Lib. 117 τὴν ἀρετήν; POxy 1664, 7 a dear person in one’s heart) τὸ ὄνομα carry the name (of Christ, or of a Christian) about (prob. as wandering preachers) IEph 7:1. The pass., in vivid imagery (Jos., Ant. 19, 46 διὰ λογισμῶν περιφερόμενος) περιφερόμενοι παντὶ ἀνέμῳ τῆς διδασκαλίας carried here and there by (any and) every wind of doctrine Eph 4:14 (or does περιφέρεσθαι mean turn around here, and is the idea of a weathervane in the background?); cp. Hb 13:9 v.l.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. sg. = singular

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

prob. prob. = probable, probably

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 808
